elevator_encounters_cornell.title: Long Way Down Cornell Notes
elevator_encounters_cornell.subtitle: Encounters, pressure, and choice
elevator_encounters_cornell.learning_target: I can track how structure and encounters shape Will's conflict.
elevator_encounters_cornell.directions: Use page references or short evidence notes. Do not copy long passages.
elevator_encounters_cornell.summary_prompt: How did today's section change your understanding of Will?
elevator_encounters_cornell.reflection_prompt: What question are you carrying forward?

guided_reading_notes.title: Long Way Down Guided Reading Notes
guided_reading_notes.subtitle: Structure, repetition, memory, rules, and choice
guided_reading_notes.learning_target: I can notice how structure builds tension and meaning.
guided_reading_notes.directions: Write brief notes or page references. Focus on patterns, pressure, and choice.
success_criteria: I connected a moment to Will's conflict.
I used page references or brief evidence notes.

discussion_notes.title: Long Way Down Discussion Notes
discussion_notes.subtitle: Class ideas and my thinking
discussion_notes.learning_target: I can record discussion ideas and revise my thinking.
discussion_notes.directions: Put a classmate's idea, question, or evidence note on the left. Put your response on the right.
discussion_notes.summary_prompt: Which discussion idea changed or sharpened your thinking?
